{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "General Manager (Breeder Farm)",
  "description": "Agricare Std Plus Limited is an agro allied company and sole distributor of standard agro-allied brands known for quality products. We have been in operation since 2006 and have contributed greatly to the success of many livestock farmers and other agro-allied establishments in Nigeria. We serve ourdiverse and unique clients all across Nigeria and beyond. With AGRICARE STD PLUS, business is more than just selling products, no matter the challenge, we focus on delivering practical and enduring results and equipping our customers with relevant information, products, and services that help them grow and thrive.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager (Breeder Farm)

Job Location: Farm Residence, Oyo (Candidate must be willing to reside on the farm premises)
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Managing Director / Executive Management

Job Summary


	The General Manager &ndash; Breeder Farm Operations will oversee the entire breeder farm operations, ensuring optimal productivity, biosecurity, bird health, hatchability performance, staff management, and operational efficiency.
	The ideal candidate must be a qualified Veterinary Doctor with extensive practical experience in breeder farm management and strong leadership capabilities within the poultry / agro-allied industry.&nbsp;
	The successful candidate will be responsible for driving operational excellence, maintaining high production standards, and ensuring profitability while adhering to best poultry management practices.


Key Responsibilities
Farm Operations Management:


	Oversee daily breeder farm operations including rearing, production, feeding, vaccination, and flock management.
	Ensure proper breeder performance in terms of fertility, hatchability, egg production, and bird health.
	Develop and implement operational plans to achieve production targets and efficiency.
	Monitor flock uniformity, feed conversion, mortality rates, and overall farm performance.
	Ensure proper environmental management including ventilation, lighting, temperature, and litter management.


Veterinary &amp; Biosecurity Management:


	Develop and enforce strict biosecurity measures across the farm.
	Supervise vaccination schedules, disease prevention programs, and treatment protocols.
	Conduct routine health assessments and ensure prompt response to disease outbreaks.
	Liaise with laboratories, veterinarians, and regulatory agencies when necessary.
	Ensure compliance with animal health and welfare standards.


Staff Supervision &amp; Leadership:


	Lead, supervise, and coordinate farm managers, supervisors, and farm workers.
	Train staff on best practices in breeder management, hygiene, safety, and operational procedures.
	Monitor staff productivity and enforce discipline and company policies.
	Foster a high-performance and teamwork-oriented culture.


Production &amp; Performance Monitoring:


	Analyze production data and prepare periodic operational reports.
	Monitor breeder KPIs including:
	
		Egg production
		Fertility rates
		Hatchability
		Mortality
		Feed consumption
		Uniformity
	
	
	Implement corrective actions where performance falls below target.


Maintenance &amp; Resource Management:


	Ensure farm equipment, machinery, and facilities are properly maintained.
	Monitor inventory levels for feed, drugs, vaccines, and farm consumables.
	Control operational costs while maintaining productivity and quality standards.
	Coordinate with procurement and maintenance teams for uninterrupted farm operations.


Compliance &amp; Administration:


	Ensure compliance with company policies, industry regulations, and safety standards.
	Prepare operational budgets and manage farm resources effectively.
	Maintain accurate farm records and documentation.
	Provide regular reports and recommendations to management.


Requirements &amp; Qualifications


	Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) is mandatory.
	8&ndash;10 years of practical experience in poultry breeder farm management.
	Strong knowledge of breeder production systems, flock management, and hatchery operations.
	Proven experience managing large-scale poultry farm operations.
	Strong leadership, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent understanding of biosecurity and disease control measures.
	Ability to work under pressure and make sound operational decisions.
	Proficiency in farm reporting and data analysis.
	Must be willing and ready to reside on the farm.


Key Competencies:


	Leadership and team management
	Strategic planning and execution
	Decision-making ability
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	High level of integrity and accountability
	Attention to detail
	Result-oriented mindset.


Compensation &amp; Benefits


	Competitive salary package.
	Accommodation provided on the farm.
	Performance-based incentives.
	Other benefits as approved by management.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications / CV to: recruit@agricareplus.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-19",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

General Manager (Breeder Farm) Job at Agricare Std Plus Limited




        
    

        
        Agricare Std Plus Limited is an agro allied company and sole distributor of standard agro-allied brands known for quality products. We have been in operation since 2006 and have contributed greatly to the success of many livestock farmers and other agro-allied establishments in Nigeria. We serve ourdiverse and unique clients all across Nigeria and beyond. With AGRICARE STD PLUS, business is more than just selling products, no matter the challenge, we focus on delivering practical and enduring results and equipping our customers with relevant information, products, and services that help them grow and thrive.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager (Breeder Farm)
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Farm Residence, Oyo (Candidate must be willing to reside on the farm premises)
Employment Type: Full-time
Reports To: Managing Director / Executive Management

Job Summary


	The General Manager – Breeder Farm Operations will oversee the entire breeder farm operations, ensuring optimal productivity, biosecurity, bird health, hatchability performance, staff management, and operational efficiency.
	The ideal candidate must be a qualified Veterinary Doctor with extensive practical experience in breeder farm management and strong leadership capabilities within the poultry / agro-allied industry. 
	The successful candidate will be responsible for driving operational excellence, maintaining high production standards, and ensuring profitability while adhering to best poultry management practices.


Key Responsibilities
Farm Operations Management:


	Oversee daily breeder farm operations including rearing, production, feeding, vaccination, and flock management.
	Ensure proper breeder performance in terms of fertility, hatchability, egg production, and bird health.
	Develop and implement operational plans to achieve production targets and efficiency.
	Monitor flock uniformity, feed conversion, mortality rates, and overall farm performance.
	Ensure proper environmental management including ventilation, lighting, temperature, and litter management.


Veterinary & Biosecurity Management:


	Develop and enforce strict biosecurity measures across the farm.
	Supervise vaccination schedules, disease prevention programs, and treatment protocols.
	Conduct routine health assessments and ensure prompt response to disease outbreaks.
	Liaise with laboratories, veterinarians, and regulatory agencies when necessary.
	Ensure compliance with animal health and welfare standards.


Staff Supervision & Leadership:


	Lead, supervise, and coordinate farm managers, supervisors, and farm workers.
	Train staff on best practices in breeder management, hygiene, safety, and operational procedures.
	Monitor staff productivity and enforce discipline and company policies.
	Foster a high-performance and teamwork-oriented culture.


Production & Performance Monitoring:


	Analyze production data and prepare periodic operational reports.
	Monitor breeder KPIs including:
	
		Egg production
		Fertility rates
		Hatchability
		Mortality
		Feed consumption
		Uniformity
	
	
	Implement corrective actions where performance falls below target.


Maintenance & Resource Management:


	Ensure farm equipment, machinery, and facilities are properly maintained.
	Monitor inventory levels for feed, drugs, vaccines, and farm consumables.
	Control operational costs while maintaining productivity and quality standards.
	Coordinate with procurement and maintenance teams for uninterrupted farm operations.


Compliance & Administration:


	Ensure compliance with company policies, industry regulations, and safety standards.
	Prepare operational budgets and manage farm resources effectively.
	Maintain accurate farm records and documentation.
	Provide regular reports and recommendations to management.


Requirements & Qualifications


	Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) is mandatory.
	8–10 years of practical experience in poultry breeder farm management.
	Strong knowledge of breeder production systems, flock management, and hatchery operations.
	Proven experience managing large-scale poultry farm operations.
	Strong leadership, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent understanding of biosecurity and disease control measures.
	Ability to work under pressure and make sound operational decisions.
	Proficiency in farm reporting and data analysis.
	Must be willing and ready to reside on the farm.


Key Competencies:


	Leadership and team management
	Strategic planning and execution
	Decision-making ability
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	High level of integrity and accountability
	Attention to detail
	Result-oriented mindset.


Compensation & Benefits


	Competitive salary package.
	Accommodation provided on the farm.
	Performance-based incentives.
	Other benefits as approved by management.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications / CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Procurement / Store Officer",
  "description": "Shalina Healthcare is one of the largest pharmaceutical companies in sub-Saharan Africa. For the last 35 years, we have made it our mission to provide quality medicines to those who need it the most. We manufacture and distribute branded prescription and over-the-counter pharmaceutical and consumer products across various therapeutic groups, including anti-malarial, antibiotics, anti-inflammatory, and nutrition. We have a track record few can match and our brands are among the most trusted in Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement / Store Officer

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos

About the job


	To Receive, inspect, and record incoming materials and finished goods.
	Ensure Stock meets the Lab specifications and fit for use.
	Do inventory/physical count and stock reconciliation activities.
	Ensure proper storage and inventory principles (FIFO/FEFO) compliance.
	Generate basic warehouse and inventory reports
	Issue stocks to relevant departments while ensuring proper documentation.
	Monitor stock levels, report stock out items and discrepancies promptly.
	Prepare dispatch documentation and coordinate outbound activities.
",

  "validThrough": "2026-06-20",

  },

Procurement / Store Officer Job at Shalina Healthcare




        
    

        
        Shalina Healthcare is one of the largest pharmaceutical companies in sub-Saharan Africa. For the last 35 years, we have made it our mission to provide quality medicines to those who need it the most. We manufacture and distribute branded prescription and over-the-counter pharmaceutical and consumer products across various therapeutic groups, including anti-malarial, antibiotics, anti-inflammatory, and nutrition. We have a track record few can match and our brands are among the most trusted in Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Procurement / Store Officer

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos

About the job
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To Receive, inspect, and record incoming materials and finished goods.
	Ensure Stock meets the Lab specifications and fit for use.
	Do inventory/physical count and stock reconciliation activities.
	Ensure proper storage and inventory principles (FIFO/FEFO) compliance.
	Generate basic warehouse and inventory reports
	Issue stocks to relevant departments while ensuring proper documentation.
	Monitor stock levels, report stock out items and discrepancies promptly.
	Prepare dispatch documentation and coordinate outbound activities.

title: Senior Software Engineer (Java) Job at HR Aid Consults

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Software Engineer (Java)",
  "description": "At HR Aid we provide consulting services in the area of Human Resource Management. Our focus is in helping our clients hire, engage and retain the best talent. We offer professional services in Talent Recruitment and Selection, HR Policy Development, Compensation &amp; Benefits Structure Development, Performance Management and HR Advisory.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Software Engineer (Java)

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (On-Site)

Job Description


	The Senior Software Engineer (Java) will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining high-quality, scalable software solutions. This role requires deep technical expertise, strong problem-solving skills, and the ability to mentor junior developers while contributing to architectural decisions.


Key Responsibilities


	Design and develop scalable Java-based applications
	Contribute to system architecture and technical design decisions
	Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code
	Review code and provide technical guidance to team members
	Collaborate with product managers and cross-functional teams
	Troubleshoot, debug, and optimize application performance
	Ensure adherence to software development best practices


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
	Minimum of 6 years of experience in professional software development using Java
	Experience in leading or mentoring other engineers is an advantage


Requirements / Skills:


	Strong proficiency in Java and related frameworks (Spring, Spring Boot)
	Experience with RESTful APIs and microservices architecture
	Solid understanding of databases (SQL and NoSQL)
	Experience with cloud-based applications and deployments
	Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills


Salary
N1,000,000 - N1,200,000 / Month (Net)

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: talent@hr-aidconsults.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-27",

Senior Software Engineer (Java) Job at HR Aid Consults




        
    

        
        At HR Aid we provide consulting services in the area of Human Resource Management. Our focus is in helping our clients hire, engage and retain the best talent. We offer professional services in Talent Recruitment and Selection, HR Policy Development, Compensation & Benefits Structure Development, Performance Management and HR Advisory.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior Software Engineer (Java)

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (On-Site)

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Senior Software Engineer (Java) will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining high-quality, scalable software solutions. This role requires deep technical expertise, strong problem-solving skills, and the ability to mentor junior developers while contributing to architectural decisions.


Key Responsibilities


	Design and develop scalable Java-based applications
	Contribute to system architecture and technical design decisions
	Write clean, maintainable, and well-documented code
	Review code and provide technical guidance to team members
	Collaborate with product managers and cross-functional teams
	Troubleshoot, debug, and optimize application performance
	Ensure adherence to software development best practices


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
	Minimum of 6 years of experience in professional software development using Java
	Experience in leading or mentoring other engineers is an advantage


Requirements / Skills:


	Strong proficiency in Java and related frameworks (Spring, Spring Boot)
	Experience with RESTful APIs and microservices architecture
	Solid understanding of databases (SQL and NoSQL)
	Experience with cloud-based applications and deployments
	Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices
	Strong problem-solving and analytical skills


Salary
N1,000,000 - N1,200,000 / Month (Net)

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Metering Engineer",
  "description": "WTS Energy - Our client, Nigeria&rsquo;s leading independent energy company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Metering Engineer
Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Job Summary


	Responsible for all metering activities which include calibration, testing and repair, new facilities testing and technical assessments to ensure accurate measurements of oil and gas and to minimize revenue losses due to meter inaccuracies.


Key Responsibilties


	Implement the requirements of the company&rsquo;s metering policy and assume the responsibilities for the applicable metering and quality management inspection tasks to ensure metering integrity and prevent loss of revenue.
	Support all production activities to meet oil and gas targets with due consideration to operating cost and technical integrity
	Provide specialist metering and associated quality management inspection (including custody transfers and fiscal metering systems) maintenance support to all assets to ensure production efficiency through accurate volume measurements.
	Review and develop maintenance plans/work instructions for these systems and carry out audits and periodic analysis of maintenance and calibration activities to enhance metering integrity and maintain license to operate through regulatory compliance
	Maintain the corporate liquid and gas metering database and maintain traceable records for all the company&rsquo;s metering system and routine recalibration to maintain technical integrity and enhance business objectives and add value to the Operations team, ensuring that produced oil &amp; gas are correctly reported and measured, providing quality and maintenance checks to improve production efficiency.
	Support field trials and evaluation of new metering instrumentation and manage the custodianship of software backup for metering and quality management inspection software to ensure continuous business activity and ensure metering integrity.
	Keep abreast of new development in measuring technology and promote best practice sharing across the company and train operators on new developments in metering to enhance human capital development.
	Ensure that periodical crude oil and gas meter proving exercise and the system recertification exercise (prover loop calibration etc.) are carried out as provided for by the regulations (especially DPR regulations). Ensure the Monthly/Quarterly system validation of the Gas fiscal metering system are properly carried out with no issues with the Authorities and third parties in support of the business
	Ensure that all fiscal, legislative and allocation metering are maintained and operated to the standards required to satisfy contractual, legislative, and operational requirements in support of the business.
	Carry out performance monitoring programs, including control charting and availability reporting and troubleshoot and investigate metering system failures utilizing root cause analysis to sustain meter reliability and integrity.
	Interface with the Well Performance &amp; Reservoir team, communicate the reliability of well test measurements and as required, assist the Process Engineer in carrying out metering-related studies concerning environment, production, etc. to improve the business
	Supervise work completed by contractors (either on site or at the contractor&rsquo;s workshop) and site teams, ensure compliance, effect necessary corrections, and submit to the Metering manager to improve metering integrity.
	Apply HSE management principles, including procedures and instructions in supervising work by contractors (either on site or at the contractor&rsquo;s workshop) and site teams to ensure compliance and effect necessary corrections in compliance to corporative objective.


Qualifications of the Representative


	A minimum of a B.Sc. Degree or HND in Instrumentation / Measurement &amp; Control / Mechanical / Electrical / Petroleum Engineering, and a minimum of 5 years of technical professional experience with significant experience in the oil and gas industry.
	Must possess good interpersonal, supervisory, communication, leading, and coordinating skills.
	Be familiar with all relevant standards (ISOs, APIs, AGAs, NUPRC, NMDPRA guidelines, etc.), types of meters, calculation methodologies, and measurement allocation and reconciliation processes.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-19",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-20",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "WTS Energy"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Rivers (PH)",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {
    "@type": "PropertyValue",
    "name": "",
    "value": "199129"
  },
  "baseSalary": {
    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 0,
      "maxValue": 0,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Metering Engineer Job at WTS Energy




        
    

        
        WTS Energy - Our client, Nigeria’s leading independent energy company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Metering Engineer
Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers

Job Summary


	Responsible for all metering activities which include calibration, testing and repair, new facilities testing and technical assessments to ensure accurate measurements of oil and gas and to minimize revenue losses due to meter inaccuracies.


Key Responsibilties
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Implement the requirements of the company’s metering policy and assume the responsibilities for the applicable metering and quality management inspection tasks to ensure metering integrity and prevent loss of revenue.
	Support all production activities to meet oil and gas targets with due consideration to operating cost and technical integrity
	Provide specialist metering and associated quality management inspection (including custody transfers and fiscal metering systems) maintenance support to all assets to ensure production efficiency through accurate volume measurements.
	Review and develop maintenance plans/work instructions for these systems and carry out audits and periodic analysis of maintenance and calibration activities to enhance metering integrity and maintain license to operate through regulatory compliance
	Maintain the corporate liquid and gas metering database and maintain traceable records for all the company’s metering system and routine recalibration to maintain technical integrity and enhance business objectives and add value to the Operations team, ensuring that produced oil & gas are correctly reported and measured, providing quality and maintenance checks to improve production efficiency.
	Support field trials and evaluation of new metering instrumentation and manage the custodianship of software backup for metering and quality management inspection software to ensure continuous business activity and ensure metering integrity.
	Keep abreast of new development in measuring technology and promote best practice sharing across the company and train operators on new developments in metering to enhance human capital development.
	Ensure that periodical crude oil and gas meter proving exercise and the system recertification exercise (prover loop calibration etc.) are carried out as provided for by the regulations (especially DPR regulations). Ensure the Monthly/Quarterly system validation of the Gas fiscal metering system are properly carried out with no issues with the Authorities and third parties in support of the business
	Ensure that all fiscal, legislative and allocation metering are maintained and operated to the standards required to satisfy contractual, legislative, and operational requirements in support of the business.
	Carry out performance monitoring programs, including control charting and availability reporting and troubleshoot and investigate metering system failures utilizing root cause analysis to sustain meter reliability and integrity.
	Interface with the Well Performance & Reservoir team, communicate the reliability of well test measurements and as required, assist the Process Engineer in carrying out metering-related studies concerning environment, production, etc. to improve the business
	Supervise work completed by contractors (either on site or at the contractor’s workshop) and site teams, ensure compliance, effect necessary corrections, and submit to the Metering manager to improve metering integrity.
	Apply HSE management principles, including procedures and instructions in supervising work by contractors (either on site or at the contractor’s workshop) and site teams to ensure compliance and effect necessary corrections in compliance to corporative objective.


Qualifications of the Representative


	A minimum of a B.Sc. Degree or HND in Instrumentation / Measurement & Control / Mechanical / Electrical / Petroleum Engineering, and a minimum of 5 years of technical professional experience with significant experience in the oil and gas industry.
	Must possess good interpersonal, supervisory, communication, leading, and coordinating skills.
	Be familiar with all relevant standards (ISOs, APIs, AGAs, NUPRC, NMDPRA guidelines, etc.), types of meters, calculation methodologies, and measurement allocation and reconciliation processes.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: Senior DevOps Engineer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	The Senior DevOps Engineer will design, implement, and maintain scalable, secure, and highly available infrastructure and deployment pipelines.
	The role focuses on automation, system reliability, and close collaboration with development teams to improve delivery speed and system stability.


Key Responsibilities


	Design and maintain CI/CD pipelines
	Automate infrastructure provisioning and configuration
	Monitor system performance, availability, and security
	Collaborate with software engineers to optimize deployments
	Troubleshoot production issues and perform root cause analysis
	&nbsp;Implement best practices for cloud security and reliability
	Mentor junior DevOps and infrastructure engineers


Requirements / Skills


	Strong experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
	Expertise in CI/CD tools (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, etc.)
	Experience with containerization and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes)
	Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation, Ansible)
	Strong Linux and scripting skills (Bash, Python)
	Solid understanding of networking, security, and system architecture


Qualifications:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field
	Minimum of 6 years of experience in DevOps, Cloud, or Infrastructure engineering
	Cloud certifications (AWS, Azure, or GCP) are an advantage.


Salary
N1,000,000 - N1,200,000 monthly.

Method of Applictaion&nbsp;

Senior DevOps Engineer Job at HR Aid Consults




        
    

        
        At HR Aid we provide consulting services in the area of Human Resource Management. Our focus is in helping our clients hire, engage and retain the best talent. We offer professional services in Talent Recruitment and Selection, HR Policy Development, Compensation & Benefits Structure Development, Performance Management and HR Advisory.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior DevOps Engineer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Senior DevOps Engineer will design, implement, and maintain scalable, secure, and highly available infrastructure and deployment pipelines.
	The role focuses on automation, system reliability, and close collaboration with development teams to improve delivery speed and system stability.


Key Responsibilities


	Design and maintain CI/CD pipelines
	Automate infrastructure provisioning and configuration
	Monitor system performance, availability, and security
	Collaborate with software engineers to optimize deployments
	Troubleshoot production issues and perform root cause analysis
	 Implement best practices for cloud security and reliability
	Mentor junior DevOps and infrastructure engineers


Requirements / Skills


	Strong experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)
	Expertise in CI/CD tools (Jenkins, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, etc.)
	Experience with containerization and orchestration (Docker, Kubernetes)
	Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation, Ansible)
	Strong Linux and scripting skills (Bash, Python)
	Solid understanding of networking, security, and system architecture


Qualifications:


	Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or related field
	Minimum of 6 years of experience in DevOps, Cloud, or Infrastructure engineering
	Cloud certifications (AWS, Azure, or GCP) are an advantage.


Salary
N1,000,000 - N1,200,000 monthly.

Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualified candidates should their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
